title: Git — Journalistic Integrity
philosophy: Social activism requires truth. Git is our immutable journalistic ledger. It holds systems accountable by tracking exactly who changed what, and when.
economics: The Baltic states, particularly Estonia, leverage cryptographic ledgers to ensure transparent e-governance. Git operates on a similar Merkle-tree foundation.
techconcept: Git commits are snapshots of your project history. Before committing, you stage changes so Git knows which edits to include in the next snapshot.
lesson: Staging lets you control exactly which changes become part of a commit. This is like deciding which facts and sources make it into a published article. Careful commits tell a clear story about how a codebase evolved.
challenge: What command stages all your current changes in Git? (Hint: git ___ .)
hint: You add files to the staging area.
